perturbation.original_text: 1.16 "Drilling Costs" means the actual costs and expenses incurred in drilling a well subject to this Agreement beginning with the initiation of preliminary site preparation activities through and including logging, testing and evaluating the well prior to recommending whether to attempt a Completion. Drilling Costs shall include, but shall not be limited to, the costs and expenses associated with permitting, preparing the site, drilling to the Objective Zone, and evaluating any Zone(s) in such well to which a Completion may be attempted, as well as any unplanned or unforeseen events such as fire, explosion, or loss of well control. Drilling Costs shall also include brokerage, abstracting, and reasonable attorney fees related to the preparation of drilling title opinions for such well. Drilling Costs shall not include the cost to plug and abandon any well, including a dry hole, and shall not include any Completion Costs.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16 "Drilling Costs" means the costs and expenses incurred in drilling a well. Drilling Costs shall include, but shall not be limited to, the costs and expenses associated with drilling to the Objective Zone. Drilling Costs shall not include any Completion Costs.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of detailed items initially included in 'Drilling Costs,' such as logging, testing, site preparation, and costs related to unforeseen events like fire or explosion, introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts Section 2.1. This section states Company shall pay 11.67% of the Drilling Costs of the Initial Well. Because the term is ambiguous, this creates uncertainty on how to properly calculate the drilling cost. The removal of these costs affects the payment obligations of the company, creating ambiguity around the allocation of costs related to unforeseen events or detailed operational stages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BP has commenced drilling operations for the Initial Well, and, except as provided elsewhere in this Agreement, BP shall continue drilling the Initial Well with due diligence to the Objective Zone and perform all logging and testing operations to which the Parties agree. Company shall pay 11.67% of the Drilling Costs of the Initial Well, regardless of whether the Initial Well is successfully drilled to the Objective Zone. BP has estimated that Drilling Costs will be approximately FOURTEEN MILLION, EIGHT HUNDRED SIXTY TWO THOUSAND DOLLARS ($14,862,000) (the "Estimated Drilling Costs") for the Initial Well. Company shall pay its share of Estimated Drilling Costs, being ONE MILLION, SEVEN HUNDRED THIRTY FOUR THOUSAND, THREE HUNDRED NINETY FIVE DOLLARS ($1,734,395), at execution of this Agreement via wire transfer according to the wiring instructions set forth in Exhibit "I", but Company will pay its share of actual Drilling Costs in accordance with this Article II and Section 5.4.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Drilling Costs' by omitting specific inclusions like logging, testing, site preparation, and costs related to unforeseen events. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which anticipates these costs being included in the 'Drilling Costs' calculation when determining the Company's 11.67% payment obligation. The narrower definition creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the actual costs the Company is responsible for, undermining the agreement's clarity and potentially leading to disputes over payment.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP and Company shall enter into an operating agreement attached hereto as Exhibit "F" (the "Operating Agreement") covering the Contract Area. The Operating Agreement shall be executed contemporaneously with the Partial Assignment but shall be effective on October 1, 2006. The Operating Agreement shall apply to all Additional Wells. In the event of any conflict between the Operating Agreement and this Agreement, this Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP and Company shall enter into an operating agreement (the "Operating Agreement") covering the Contract Area. The Operating Agreement shall apply to all Additional Wells. In the event of any conflict between the Operating Agreement and this Agreement, this Agreement shall govern.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause 'attached hereto as Exhibit "F"' from Section 5.5 creates an in-text contradiction because there is no longer a clearly defined reference point for the Operating Agreement to be entered into. Section 6.2 references the Operating Agreement, but without Exhibit F the nature of the operating agreement becomes undefined, which then leads to further uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Section 6.1, if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, this Agreement shall continue for so long as the Operating Agreement remains in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.5 omits the reference to Exhibit "F", which previously defined the Operating Agreement. The contradicted text in Section 6.2 relies on the existence and definition of the Operating Agreement. Without Exhibit F, the Operating Agreement becomes undefined, creating a contradiction because the continuation of the agreement hinges on an undefined document, making the enforcement unclear and potentially leading to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means a single operation intended to complete a well as a producer of oil and/or gas in one or more Zone(s), including, but not limited to, the setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means an operation intended to potentially produce oil and/or gas.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of specific actions, such as 'setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing' from the definition of Completion undermines the clarity for the Completion Costs. Because the removal is ambiguous, this also undermines the clarity for Section 2.3, affecting the payment obligations of the company, creating uncertainty on how to properly calculate the completion cost. In specific: 'Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If BP proposes to Complete the Initial Well and Company timely elects to participate in such Completion attempt in accordance with Section 2.2(A), Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well and 8.75% of the cost of any newly acquired surface equipment associated with the Initial Well beyond the wellhead connections (including but not limited to stock tanks, separators, treaters, pumping equipment, piping, and metering devices).
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed list of actions that constituted 'Completion'. The changed text significantly narrows this definition to a mere 'operation intended to potentially produce oil and/or gas.' This omission of specific actions, directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, 'Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well'. The removal of specifics such as 'setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing' creates ambiguity regarding what constitutes Completion Costs. Since Completion Costs is a key payment obligation, the revised definition creates uncertainty and thus a contradiction.
